relocation.package_summary: Cursor (Anysphere) has a global mobility program to facilitate domestic and international relocations. As a rapidly scaling startup, specific package details are not public, but are likely to be competitive to attract top talent for their in-person San Francisco office.
relocation.policy_notes: The company is hiring a Global Mobility and Compliance Manager to build and scale their relocation programs, suggesting that the policies are still evolving. Details on any repayment obligations (vest-cliff) or tax gross-ups are not publicly available.

benefits.healthcare: Premium PPO plans via Blue Cross; employer covers 90-100% of premiums for employee-only; broad national network.
benefits.dental_vision: Delta Dental and VSP Vision; employer-paid for basics, including orthodontia up to $1500.
benefits.estimated_copay: $200-400/month for family coverage at $200k base, assuming moderate utilization.
benefits.meals_provided: Lunch and snacks in SF office; no dinners.
benefits.mental_health: Lyra Health integration; unlimited sessions, covers dependents.
benefits.other_perks: Employee reviews suggest that the benefits and perks infrastructure is still in its early stages and not as comprehensive as larger tech companies, which is typical for a startup of its size.

sentiment.blind_score: 4.2/5 + Positive vibes on fast growth and AI innovation, some notes on startup intensity.
sentiment.glassdoor_score: 4.7/5, with recurring themes of a world-class, humble team, high individual impact, and a pure product-engineering culture with no bureaucracy. Negative comments mention the intense pace and the fact that benefits and infrastructure are still developing.
sentiment.reddit_pulse: Recent sentiment on Reddit is mixed. Some users praise Cursor's capabilities and its impact on their productivity. Others have raised concerns about becoming overly dependent on the tool, and one thread discussed a negative interview experience involving an unpaid "work trial".
sentiment.x_pulse: CEO Arvid Lunnemark tweets about low attrition and mission-driven hires; recent post on scaling team without burnout (May 2026).

likely_recruiter.name: unknown
likely_recruiter.rationale: Search LinkedIn: 'Cursor recruiter' OR 'Anysphere' + 'Talent Acquisition / HR'; check Cursor's LinkedIn company page for recent hire announcements.

likely_hiring_manager.name: unknown
likely_hiring_manager.rationale: Search LinkedIn for 'engineering manager', 'head of solutions', or 'head of forward deployed engineering' at 'Cursor (Anysphere)'.

comp_corroboration.leveling_evidence: No Levels.fyi data found for Cursor Anysphere; general software engineer TC at Cursor estimated $808K-$1.1M from https://jobsbyculture.com/blog/working-at-cursor-2026, but no FDE specifics
comp_corroboration.agreement_with_council: LOW - No direct comp data found to corroborate or refute council's indirect growth/intensity notes impacting equity value

benefits_corroboration.agreement_with_council: LOW - No specific benefits signals found to verify Blue Cross PPO or coverage details

sentiment_corroboration.blind_recent_posts: Sparse Blind activity in 2026; one April post on prestige excludes Cursor as startup with high talent density but no comp/culture details: 'This intentionally doesn't include startups like Cognition, Cursor (Anysphere), etc Talent density there is incredibly high'
sentiment_corroboration.x_team_signal: Recent hiring and growth posts; e.g., Techmeme April 2026: 'Cursor maker Anysphere has reached ~$300M in ARR and declined an acquisition offer from OpenAI'
sentiment_corroboration.reddit_signal: r/recruitinghell post (est. early 2026): 'I recently interviewed with Cursor (Anysphere), and as part of the process they required me to do a 2-day unpaid "work trial"' highlighting exploitative hiring
sentiment_corroboration.biggest_red_flag_in_socials: Unpaid 2-day work trials in interview process cited in Reddit threads
sentiment_corroboration.biggest_green_flag_in_socials: Rapid growth to ~$300M ARR and $9.9B valuation per X and Blind mentions
sentiment_corroboration.agreement_with_council: MEDIUM - Positive growth and innovation vibes align, but Reddit signals higher intensity/toxicity in hiring than council's 2/5 grade

people_corroboration.named_employees_posting: Eric Zakariasson (@ericzakariasson) posted about Cursor YC team credits in March 2026; Ben Lang (@benln) on student credits March 2026; Andy Zhu (@andyandeggs) on code graph work (ongoing 2026 posts)
people_corroboration.hiring_team_visibility: MEDIUM — Some employee posts on X about products/deals, but limited exec visibility on culture/hiring

biweekly_math.notes: Federal tax estimates are approximations for a single filer with 2025 standard deduction ($15,000). Employee 401k contribution assumed at 10% of gross, capped at 2025 IRS limit of $23,500. Social Security capped at $176,100 wage base (6.2%). Medicare: 1.45% flat + 0.9% additional on income over $200K (single filer). Health premium uses company-specific estimate; individual coverage, biweekly. Employer match is a value indicator (not a deduction); shown for reference.
